name: Ministry of Children and Family Development

goals.short_name: Children and youth in B.C. are protected from abuse and neglect

main_services.name: Report and respond to a child protection concern
Take part in a child protection legal process

goals.short_name: Children and youth have stable, connected caregiving and permanency options

main_services.name: Become a caregiver for a child or youth in care

goals.short_name: Children, youth and families get the mental health, disability and family supports they need to thrive

main_services.name: Get mental health support for a child or youth
Get support for a child or youth with a disability or support needs
Get support to keep families together and help youth transition to adulthood

goals.short_name: Indigenous children, youth and families exercise jurisdiction over their own child and family services

main_services.name: Exercise Indigenous jurisdiction over child and family services

acts.full_name: Child, Family and Community Service Act
acts.summary: The legislative authority for the ministry's child protection services. Sets out the duty to report abuse or neglect, the Director of Child Protection's delegated authority, foster home approval and standards, mediation, and youth agreements. Amended in 2018/2019 to strengthen Indigenous engagement and information-sharing in child welfare matters.

sections.title: Duty to report
sections.description: Everyone in B.C. has a legal duty to report if they believe a child or youth may be abused, neglected, or otherwise in need of protection.

services.name: Report suspected child abuse or neglect
services.ms_name: Report and respond to a child protection concern

sections.title: Delegation of the Director's authority
sections.description: The Minister designates the Director of Child Protection, who delegates provision of child protection services to social workers and delegated Aboriginal/Indigenous agencies province-wide.

services.name: Child protection social work and delegated authority administration

sections.title: Mediation and dispute resolution
sections.description: Provides for voluntary mediation between families and the ministry as an alternative to court in child protection matters.

services.name: Take part in child protection mediation
services.ms_name: Take part in a child protection legal process

sections.title: Youth agreements
sections.description: Authorizes agreements providing support to youth aged 16-18 who cannot safely live at home and are not in continuing care.

services.name: Enter into a Youth Agreement for independent living support
services.ms_name: Take part in a child protection legal process

sections.title: Approval of foster homes
sections.description: Social workers hold delegated authority to approve foster homes for children coming into the care of the ministry.

services.name: Become a foster caregiver
services.ms_name: Become a caregiver for a child or youth in care

sections.title: Standards for foster homes
sections.description: Sets minimum standards for the care and supervision of children and youth living in foster homes.

services.name: Get payments and support as a current foster caregiver
services.ms_name: Become a caregiver for a child or youth in care

sections.ref: Indigenous engagement and information sharing (2018/2019 amendments)
sections.title: Indigenous engagement and information sharing
sections.description: 2018 amendments (Bill 26) require early engagement with Indigenous communities and information-sharing to keep Indigenous children and families connected to their communities.

services.name: Establish a coordination agreement for Indigenous jurisdiction over child and family services
services.ms_name: Exercise Indigenous jurisdiction over child and family services

acts.summary: Governs adoption in British Columbia, including placement for adoption, consents, care and guardianship, court proceedings, interprovincial and intercountry adoptions, and openness agreements.

sections.title: Adopting from foster care
sections.description: Sets out placement for adoption of children in government care by a director or adoption agency.

services.name: Adopt a child from foster care

sections.title: Placement for adoption and consents
sections.description: Governs who may place and receive a child for adoption, and the consents required from birth parents.

services.name: Adopt an infant through a licensed adoption agency

sections.title: Interprovincial and intercountry adoptions
sections.description: Governs adoptions where a child is brought into B.C. from another province or country, including Hague Convention intercountry adoptions.

services.name: Adopt a child from another country

sections.title: Court proceedings for adoption
sections.description: Sets out who may apply to court to adopt a child, notice requirements, and the adoption order process.

services.name: Adopt a relative's child or your partner's stepchild
